title: Level 188.65
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|date=06/05/2026|"I do not believe that this deserves deletion. First of all, this DOES have similarities with Level 188 because it was moved to a sublevel slot. However, there ARE some key differences. For example, 188 is finite and 188.65 is infinite, 188.65 has more mental hazards rather than physical like 188, the windows of 188.65 operate completely differently, 188.65 is a larger and more detailed page compared to 188, et cetera. As for the issue of duplicate images, I could not find any, so if you could point out which ones are duplicates or not I would be grateful. If you want a more comprehensive list of differences feel free to message me." The first image is incredibly similar to Level 188's; before saying it is a Level 188 sublevel, they still have to have some sort of major difference other than just having a sky. Alright, the concept. It is, in fact, incredibly similar to Level 188. I can pretty much describe it as: "Level 188, but it generates in an infinite pentagon shape". There is nothing too memorable other than the pentagonal generation. The page also suffers from numerous writing issues, i have created a list here: {{Collapse|initial=Open Writing Issues|final=Close Writing Issues|body= * "Level 188.65 manifests as an infinite series of hotels[1]." This sentence is rather blunt; it just tells the reader what it is. Yes, it's a description, but it should not be as blunt or straightforward as this. * "The wanderer starts at the same center hotel every time" Informal, this can also be pushed down in the description. More important elements of the level should be pushed up, while less important details like this are pushed down. * "-the Hallways and the Rooms." Hallways and rooms are common nouns and should not be capitalised. * "The Hallways are simply hallways connecting all the rooms on each floor together." They describe themselves in the first five words; you could've gone straight into describing their details rather than saying they are "simply hallways". They are called hallways. * "These are often calming if bland." This is never elaborated on. How? * "While the Rooms are certainly mundane, they pose no threats to wanderers, being safe areas to rest and relax." Again, a common noun. Also, why would anyone be uncertain that they are mundane? It just seems like an unusual choice of words. * "In the center of each and every hotel is a large-" Each and every is redundant. * "The remaining 4/9ths is brick floors, with a small circular section against the wall for more greenery." Grammar. * "If one stays within the level for extended periods of time[3], one may encounter a disease known as Hotel Sickness." Encounter is a weird word to use here. Why not contract? The coordinate system in Notable Phenomena is weird; it's human-made after all. Also, its coordinate: not co-ordinate. Overall, the coordinate system seems incredibly overcomplicated. Why not use a different coordinate system, like the simple XYZ, or longitude and latitude? It seems incredibly random why they would organise it in that way, instead of using a simpler system. * "This level formerly held only humans and Facelings. However, during The Flicker, Windows began rapidly flooding the level. Additionally, there is a level-exclusive entity featured below." Windows and Facelings are proper nouns and should not be capitalised. Peripherals are level-specific; it is never implied that these also appear on 188's sublevels in [[Level 188]]. Also, why would they be sub-entities of [[Entity 2]]? Its inconclusive on if they are related to [[Entity 2]]. * "-frequently described as roughly 7 feet tall." Frequently doesn't flow too well here; why not use often instead? * "This entity targets the peripherals of a given wanderer's vision. This entity sticks to the peripherals, not displaying itself in any way other than at the edge of one's vision." Describing the same thing twice. Verbosity. * "-sends people hurtling towards the Wretch Cycle, or worse." Informal. * "The best way to counter this is to see past your peripheral vision." Seeing past your peripheral vision is impossible. It's your peripheral, while you do explain using mirrors, it can be worded better, to be less confusing. * "-within any human's line of sight-" This is inconsistent with your previous explanation, they only appear to one wanderer at a time."This entity targets the peripherals of a given wanderer's vision." * "-15 seconds of unbroken eye contact-" Inconsistency. * "-but it is believed it is a group, as multiple attacks have been detected at the same time." This implies they AREN'T a collective, more so being able to cooperate and work together. Also, detected is an unusual choice of word here. I honestly wish there was more elaboration on the groups, instead of just variables. The random change from exits and entrances into Ingresses and Egresses feels out of place. * "-red lighting and more antique windows-" Feels strangely worded, why not more ornate windows? |num=1}} Do not remove this template, doing so will result in staff being contacted. }}

2Ψ
|header=Class:|safety=Safe|security=Secure|entity=Manageable Hostile Presence|environment=Minor Psychological Hazards|bg-image=188 class image.png|bg-size=1535|text-color=|chevron=Ͱ}} {{Blurimage|file=Hyperbolic Hotel.png|width=250px|hovertext=The Hyperbolic Hotel.|caption=An image of'''Level 188.65'''.}} {{Blurimage|file=Level188from6th.png|hovertext=🪟|width=250px|caption=Another image of '''Level 188.65''', depicting a small colony.}} {{Blurbackground|file=Backrooms-Level-188-Holiday-Inn-Express-London-Heathrow-T4.png}} {{Blurimage|file=Benign Hotel.png|width=250px|hovertext=Mundane yet relaxing.|caption=An image of a hotel hallway in '''Level 188.65'''.}} {{Blurimage|file=Level 188 image hotel room.webp|width=250px|hovertext=Rest.|caption=An image of a rather bare hotel room in '''Level 188.65'''.}} __NOTOC____NOEDITSECTION____NOCC__{{DISPLAYTITLE: }}{{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Merriweather:ital,opsz,wght@0,18..144,300..900;1,18..144,300..900&family=VT323&display=swap'); @keyframes fade { 0%,100% { opacity: 1; } 50%{ opacity:0; } } .t1 { animation: fade 5s linear infinite; } .t2 { animation: fade 5s linear infinite; animation-delay:2.5s; opacity:0; } .image { display:inline-block; box-shadow: 0px 0px 10px #FFFFFF; transition: transform 0.35s ease; } .image:hover { transform: scale(1.05); } }} Level 188.65 is a sublevel of [[Level 188]]. ==Description== Level 188.65 manifests as an infinite series of hotelsDubious, see Notable Phenomena.. The wanderer starts at the same center hotel every time, with a handful of exceptions. These hotels are typically 5 to 6 floors high, and consist of two areas, the Hallways and the Rooms. The Hallways are simply hallways connecting all the rooms on each floor together. These halls usually have minimal decoration, save for chandeliers and abstract paintings. Each hallway has its own unique color scheme. Notably, certain hallways may have stairwells, these connect each floor together. The Rooms are the hotel rooms that permeate the hallways at regular intervals. These are often calming if bland. They have a small kitchenette, grey carpeting, a mini fridge, a queen-size bed with white furnishings, a few chairs and tables of different materials, and a large window with grey curtains. While the Rooms are certainly mundane, they pose no threats to wanderers, being safe areas to rest and relax. Rarely, hotel rooms may have severe deviations. Typically, these deviations involve the combining of multiple rooms or the furnishings of rooms becoming more advanced. These rooms are referred to as 'deluxe rooms' by locals. All rooms share the same effect, which causes supplies within the rooms to slowly replenish over a 24 hour period. This allows extended stays and potentially larger and self-serving colonies, although supplies within rooms are usually enough for only three people, and for larger colonies requires foraging further and further away. In the center of each and every hotel is a large, pentagonal-shaped courtyard. Each courtyard has 5/9th of the space dedicated to greenery, with four concrete walkways headed perpendicular to each other against a small wooden platform in the center. The remaining 4/9ths is brick floors, with a small circular section against the wall for more greenery. These courtyards are open and wide, with an area of roughly 3,066m2In plaintext, this is three thousand and sixty six meters squared.. They are hotspots for wanderer activity, notably hosting centers of commerce. The courtyards' ceiling is not open air, but a painted blue sky with a defined brick pattern. Each hotel is roughly ≈1.07 acres. ==Notable Phenomena== It should be noted that although it would appear otherwise, the geometry of Level 188.65 is [[Phenomenon 14|non-Euclidean]]. The infinite series of hotels have no delineation, leaving only the Courtyards as a frame of reference. It is assumed that the shape of the hotels is pentagonal. However, one cannot tile a plane using pentagons, so in regular geometry there would be diamond-shaped gaps. However, because there are no visible gaps, it is theorized that the level occurs on a hyperbolic plane, allowing the hotels to tile infinitely. If one stays within the level for extended periods of timeRoughly 3 days., one may encounter a disease known as Hotel Sickness. Hotel Sickness manifests as a persistent headache amongst other body aches, feelings of fatigue or dizziness, and a large feeling commonly identified as home sickness towards a certain level. This can be any level, regardless of safety. The effects are cleared once one visits the chosen level. However, the physical and mental effects are noticeably mitigated by staying amongst a group, creating a feeling of 'home' that displaces the effects of Hotel Sickness. While the mental symptom is completely erased, the physical symptoms merely have less severity. ===Co-ordinate system=== Due to the distinctly non-Euclidean but nonetheless stable geometry of Level 188.65, a co-ordinate system was devised. This co-ordinate system uses the initial hotel's courtyard as a frame of reference due to the central location of the courtyard. The center courtyard's respective hotel leads to five others seamlessly, labeled 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 by the co-ordinate system. The central courtyard is labeled 0, or scarcely 'home'. From this point onward, due to the hyperbolic nature of the plane there will always be five possible directions the wanderer can go, excluding backwards. These will be labeled x1, x2, x3, x4, and x5, with x being the chosen initial pentagon. An example of a co-ordinate is going to pentagon 5, then going the farthest left option and then the up-left direction will lead them to Hotel 512. When adding numbers, 1 is the leftmost, 5 is the rightmost, and 3 is directly forward. Multiple co-ordinates can be assigned to the same hotel, for example going to 1 and going right has the same results as going to 2 and going left, so the pentagon can be called both 15 and 21. ==Entities== This level formerly held only humans and [[Entity 9|Facelings]]. However, during [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]], [[Entity 2|Windows]] began rapidly flooding the level. Additionally, there is a level-exclusive entity featured below.
Addendum: Peripherals ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Level-188-real-entity.png|caption=Rendering of a peripheral.|hovertext=This is only a rendering, as it is impossible to photograph or record this entity.}} Entity 2-A, frequently known as Peripherals, are an entity only found to inhabit [[Level 188]] and its sublevels. This entity manifests as a shadowy humanoid, frequently described as roughly 7 feet tall. This entity targets the peripherals of a given wanderer's vision. This entity sticks to the peripherals, not displaying itself in any way other than at the edge of one's vision. If a Peripheral stays in one's vision for too long, affected individuals will begin having feelings of anxiety, paranoia and scopophobia. These feelings will slowly increase in intensity, which heavily impacts one's mental well-being and often sends people hurtling towards [[Phenomenon 35|the Wretch Cycle]], or [[Phenomenon_25|worse]]. This usually takes between 8 and 12 hours to take effect. The best way to counter this is to see past your peripheral vision. This can be done by asking others to watch your back or surrounding yourself in mirrors. If the entity is within any human's line of sight, it will disappear. It will usually re-appear after this stops, but after roughly 15 seconds of unbroken eye contact it will give up and move to a different victim. It is unknown whether the Peripherals are one individual entity or a group of different entities, but it is believed it is a group, as multiple attacks have been detected at the same time. It is believed to be related to the shadowy figures sometimes spotted amongst the sceneries from [[Entity 2|Windows]], but this is merely a theory.
==History== {{Blurimage|file=Download (21).png|caption=A particular hotel 20 co-ordinate places away from the center. Notice the [[Phenomenon 11|instability]].}}Level 188.65 was discovered around 1903 by a group of M.E.G. operatives labeled Group ChiDue to the lower quality of records at the time, the only mention of Group Chi is in relation to Level 188.65.. The inhabitants of the Aperture Hotelier were already within the level by this time. In 1958, the level's population reached 100 recorded humans. In 1988, the co-ordinate system was devised to be able to navigate easier, and in response the Newcomer's Association was formed at 0. In early 1998, 2211121 was discovered with pipes lining the entirety of the hotel's walls, including the courtyard. This was initially believed to be an exit to [[Level 2]] or [[Level 3]], however on the 9th of September, 1998, the pipes burst, flooding the courtyard of 2211121. This later became known as the exit to [[Level 7]]. Later, 5221 became the exit to [[Level 366]] after a slow period of transition. During [[Phenomenon 12|The Flicker]], [[Entity 2|Windows]] began to rapidly populate the level, an infestation that has not been cleared to this day. In an exploration in 2019, individual wanderers met up and trekked roughly one gigameter, reaching a hotel over 20 co-ordinate places away from the center. Around the 15-coordinate mark, [[Phenomenon 11]] began to take hold; however the usual negative effects of the phenomenon were nowhere to be found, leaving only the glitched appearance. ==Colonies and Outposts== ===Newcomer's Association=== *Located at 0. *Group dedicated to introducing new wanderers to the level. *Roughly 12 members, including 1 [[Entity 9|Faceling]]. *Survives off of donations from other colonies. *Open to trades, but closed to new memberships. ===Hotel Staff=== *Located in a triangle between 1122, 11212 and 22122. *Group dedicated to building an adequate society. *Roughly 28 members, all human. *Survives off of foraging and supplies from other levels. *Open to trading and new memberships. ===M.E.G. Base Windowlodge=== *Located at 422112. *Outpost dedicated to researching the level and its geometry. *Roughly 8 members, all human. *Survives off of foraging and supplies from [[Base Alpha]]. *Open to trading, closed to new memberships ===The Aperture Hotelier=== *Nomadic, strays from othe groups. *Claims to be the owners and operators of the Hotel, as well as its staff. *Roughly 12 members, all [[Entity 9|Facelings]]. *Survives off of foraging. *Closed to trading and memberships, hostile to non-Facelings. ===Residents=== *Small groupings of residents, farthest away one is 12 co-ordinate digits away. *Dedicated to exploring the level, documenting new hotels and generally thriving. *Usually 8-20 members per group, overall roughly 500 people follow this lifestyle. *Survive off of foraging. *Closed to trading, but open to membership. ==Thresholds== ===Ingresses=== *Random, out-of-place windows interspersed throughout most levels arrive here. *Scarcely, one may find courtyards in [[Level 5]] or [[Level 366]]. Traveling through causes the courtyards to loop and slowly transition into 0. **While this is theorized to be possible for all hotel-related levels, [[Level 5]] and [[Level 366]] are the only documented cases. *Three cases have reported that abnormally large eyes inconsistent with the other sculptures of [[Level 5.55]] open upon close proximity and deposit one into 0. Eyes are the ''windows'' into the soul, after all. *In levels in [[Backrooms Wiki:Levels 600-699|the 600s range]], large open spaces will occasionally appear. Entering one of these spaces will cause one to black out and wake up in a Room. *There are at least 2 cases of wanderers no-clipping here from [[The Frontrooms|Reality]], both of which in close proximity to London. ===Egresses=== *Windows spread throughout the level may scarcely lead to other levels. The level picked is random, although if one is infected with untreated Hotel Sickness they have a roughly 66% chance to deposit one in the related level. *Traveling to 2211121 leads one to a flooded courtyard, which transitions into [[Level 7]], or potentially other aquatic levels. *Traveling to 5221 leads one to a courtyard with red lighting and more antique windows, which transitions into [[Level 366]]. **Scarcely, this can also lead one to [[Level 5]]. *This level may randomly fade into [[Level 188]]. **Additionally, 5221 has a slight chance to direct one to [[Level 188.1]], and 2211121 also has a slight chance to direct one to [[Level 188.8]]. {{Collapse|initial=«Expand Author Information»|final=»Contract Author Information«|num=188|body= *Welcome to [[User:JakitoFan|my]] level! I'm the sole author here. *Okay, so... did you like the level? Say something in the comments about it. *I'm always looking for ways to improve. *Check out [[User:JakitoFan/Verzephobia|a random sandbox of mine!]] *Also, something you should know is that all the photos were taken in one place. It's called the Holiday Inn Express London Heathrow T4 by IHG. It is located at 51°27'32"N 0°26'35"W. The first image featured was edited to have a sky ceiling. *I hope you have a good day! :D *P.S., this page was posted when Level 188's most recent edit was the first of January, 2026. If you find the level is too similar to a post-rewrite Level 188, note that this level was in fact based on an independent rewrite but blossomed into its own level, similar to how The Broken was originally just a rewrite for The Whole, but blossomed into its own page. Later, this was changed so 648 (the level's original classification) become 188.5 to resolve the issue of concept. *Also, before you complain about length, this page has a byte count five times higher than [[Level 188]], which is one of the most famous Backrooms levels in history. [[Category:Safe]][[Category:Class 2Ψ]][[Category:Levels]][[Category:Humanoid]][[Category:Entities]][[Category:Groups]][[Category:Glitched Levels]][[Category:Sub-Levels]][[Category:Mental Hazards]] }} {{Audio|file=Chill music.ogg|volume=0.6|loop=yes|secrettext=Calm.|toggle=1}} {{Nobadge}} {{CSS|content= .fandom-community-header__image img { opacity:0; } .fandom-community-header__image::before { content:''; background:url(https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/backrooms/images/3/32/648logo.png/revision/latest?cb=20260206155544&format=original) no-repeat; width:100px; height:112px; background-size:100px; position: absolute; transition: 0s; } }} {{CSS|content= .compass-wiki-badge { display: none; } }} {{CSS|content= .fandom-community-header__community-name::before{ content: " "; color: #FFFFFF; } .fandom-community-header__community-name { font-size: 0; } .fandom-community-header__community-name::before { font-size: 24px; content: 'The Hyperbolic Hotel - The Backrooms Wiki'; line-height: 3; position: relative; top: 18px; } }} {{Redirect|Prev=188.1|Next=188.8}}
